_source: Master Electrician field guides, Fluke application notes, Industrial troubleshooting manuals
_note: Long-tail electrical troubleshooting. Specific diagnostic procedures, meter readings, failure modes.

120v_outlet.hot_to_neutral: Should read 118-122V; <110V indicates voltage drop problem
120v_outlet.hot_to_ground: Should read 118-122V; if significantly different from hot-neutral, indicates neutral problem
120v_outlet.neutral_to_ground: Should read 0-2V; >2V indicates loose neutral or shared neutral issue
120v_outlet.reversed_polarity: Hot in neutral slot; reads 120V both ways; dangerous; rewire immediately

240v_circuit.high_leg_delta: Wild leg (orange wire) reads 208V to neutral; 240V to other legs

continuity_testing.procedure: Power OFF; discharge capacitors; test conductor end-to-end
continuity_testing.good_reading: <0.5 ohms for short runs; <2 ohms for 100ft run
continuity_testing.intermittent: Wiggle wire while testing; fluctuating reading indicates damaged conductor

insulation_resistance_megger.minimum_acceptable: >1 megohm (1000 kΩ) for new work; >0.5 megohm for old work
insulation_resistance_megger.concerning_readings: <0.5 megohm indicates insulation degradation; investigate before energizing
insulation_resistance_megger.procedure: Disconnect load; isolate circuit; test conductor to ground; duration: 1 minute
insulation_resistance_megger.polarization_index: Ratio of 10-minute to 1-minute reading; should be >2.0 for good insulation

current_measurement.clamp_meter_use: Clamp around single conductor only; clamping full cable reads net current (cancels out)
current_measurement.balanced_load: Measure each leg; should be within 10% of each other; >20% difference indicates problem
current_measurement.identifying_shared_neutral: Clamp neutral wire; if reading higher than expected, multiple circuits sharing neutral
current_measurement.inrush_vs_running: Motor inrush 6-8x running current; use min/max capture to measure startup surge

immediate_trip.diagnostic: Disconnect all loads; if still trips, fault in wiring; if doesn't trip, fault in load
immediate_trip.thermal_vs_magnetic: Immediate = magnetic trip (short); delayed = thermal trip (overload)

trips_after_time.likely_cause: Overload; circuit drawing >100% rated current continuously
trips_after_time.calculation: Add up all connected loads; if >80% breaker rating continuously, circuit overloaded
trips_after_time.solution: Redistribute loads to other circuits or upgrade wire and breaker

nuisance_tripping.afci_issues: Loose connections, damaged wire, incompatible devices (some dimmers, LED drivers)
nuisance_tripping.gfci_issues: Ground fault (wet location, damaged tool), shared neutral, improper wiring
nuisance_tripping.diagnostic: Plug in loads one-by-one to isolate offending device

breaker_hot_to_touch.cause: Loose connection at breaker terminal or bus bar
breaker_hot_to_touch.resistance_heating: Even small resistance (0.5Ω) at 20A = 200W heat = fire risk
breaker_hot_to_touch.action: Power down panel; inspect connection; torque to spec (check breaker listing); replace if damaged

whole_house_flicker.loose_service_connection: Utility connection, meter base, or service entrance; call utility and electrician
whole_house_flicker.undersized_service: Voltage sag when large load starts (AC, dryer); measure voltage drop; upgrade service if chronic
whole_house_flicker.shared_neutral_problem: Lights brighten/dim when other circuits turn on; lost neutral creates 240V across 120V loads

single_circuit_flicker.loose_connection: At switch, outlet, or fixture; creates resistance and arcing; fire hazard
single_circuit_flicker.diagnostic: IR camera shows hot spot at loose connection; or use voltage drop test under load
single_circuit_flicker.backstabbed_outlets: Common failure point; rewire with screw terminals

led_specific_flicker.incompatible_dimmer: LED needs electronic low-voltage dimmer; magnetic dimmer causes flicker
led_specific_flicker.driver_issue: Cheap LED drivers; upgrade to quality bulbs with better power supply filtering
led_specific_flicker.minimum_load: Some dimmers need 25W minimum; single LED <10W may flicker; add dummy load or upgrade dimmer

outlets_not_working.gfci_tripped: Test/reset button popped; check all GFCIs on circuit (first GFCI protects downstream outlets)
outlets_not_working.open_neutral: Outlet reads 0V hot-neutral but 120V hot-ground; neutral disconnected upstream
outlets_not_working.lost_neutral_shared: Outlet reads 0V or 240V (if shared neutral lost); extremely dangerous; correct immediately
outlets_not_working.backstab_failure: Outlet worked then stopped; wiggle plug and outlet; loose backstab connection; rewire
outlets_not_working.series_wiring_failure: One bad outlet takes out all downstream; check outlets in sequence; find failed connection point

hums_but_doesnt_start.single_phase_lost: 240V motor on single 120V leg; bad breaker or lost phase; check voltage leg-to-leg and leg-to-neutral
hums_but_doesnt_start.capacitor_failure: Start capacitor open; motor has no starting torque; test capacitor with capacitance meter
hums_but_doesnt_start.mechanical_binding: Seized bearing; excessive load; disconnect load and try motor; if starts, mechanical problem

thermal_overload_trips.overload_condition: Motor drawing >nameplate FLA; check current; reduce load or upgrade motor
thermal_overload_trips.low_voltage: Voltage <90% rated causes high current draw; check voltage at motor under load
thermal_overload_trips.cooling_issue: Blocked vents; failed fan; motor overheats even at rated load; clean and inspect
thermal_overload_trips.incorrect_overload_setting: Overload set too low; should be 115-125% motor FLA

reverse_rotation.three_phase: Swap any two legs at motor or starter
reverse_rotation.single_phase: Reverse rotation requires reversing start winding; consult motor diagram; some motors not reversible

voltage_sag.definition: Voltage drops 10-20% for short duration (cycles to seconds)
voltage_sag.causes: Motor starting, utility fault clearing, transformer tap change
voltage_sag.effects: Lights dim, computer UPS switches to battery, motor stalls
voltage_sag.mitigation: Voltage regulator, soft starter for large motors, UPS for sensitive loads

harmonics.sources: Switch-mode power supplies, VFDs, LED drivers, computer equipment
harmonics.effects: Neutral conductor overheating (triplen harmonics add in neutral), transformer overheating, nuisance breaker trips
harmonics.measurement: Total Harmonic Distortion (THD); >5% THD problematic; >20% severe
harmonics.mitigation: K-rated transformers, derate neutral, harmonic filters, phase-shifting transformers

transients.lightning_induced: kV spikes; sub-microsecond duration; destroys electronics
transients.switching_transients: Breaker or contactor operation; few hundred volts; nanosecond to microsecond
transients.protection: Whole-house surge protector (Type 1/2 at panel); Type 3 at sensitive equipment; MOV or TVS diodes

steps: Turn on all devices on circuit to maximum setting
Measure current with clamp meter on hot conductor
Compare to breaker rating; should be <80% for continuous loads
If >80%, redistribute loads or upgrade circuit
circuit_load_check.example: 20A kitchen circuit: toaster 1200W, microwave 1500W, kettle 1500W = 4200W / 120V = 35A; grossly overloaded; never run all three simultaneously

voltage_drop_field_test.procedure: Measure voltage at panel (V1); measure at furthest outlet under full load (V2); VD% = (V1-V2)/V1 × 100
voltage_drop_field_test.example: Panel: 120.5V; outlet under 15A load: 116.2V; drop = 4.3V = 3.6%; borderline; consider upsizing wire

insulation_resistance_method.step_1: Megger circuit from panel; reads low resistance to ground
insulation_resistance_method.step_2: Disconnect circuit at midpoint; megger each half separately
insulation_resistance_method.step_3: Fault will show in one half; repeat subdivision until fault isolated to single run
insulation_resistance_method.resolution: Can locate fault to specific junction box or damaged section

phase_sequence_testing.why_it_matters: Incorrect sequence causes motor reverse rotation, malfunctions
phase_sequence_testing.tools: Phase rotation meter; should indicate A-B-C or clockwise rotation
phase_sequence_testing.correcting: Swap any two phases at service or at motor to reverse rotation

phase_balance.measurement: Clamp each phase; currents should be within 10% of average
phase_balance.unbalanced_loads: Single-phase loads distributed unevenly; causes neutral current, transformer overheating
phase_balance.unbalanced_voltage: One phase significantly different; indicates utility problem or bad transformer; call utility
phase_balance.effect_on_motors: Voltage imbalance causes current imbalance squared; 2% V imbalance → 20% I imbalance → overheating

open_phase_detection.symptoms: Three-phase motor runs on two phases; draws 2x current, reduced power, severe overheating
open_phase_detection.voltage_readings: Dead leg reads 0V to other legs (if open at source); may read partial voltage through motor windings
open_phase_detection.current_readings: Two legs draw excessive current; third leg reads zero
open_phase_detection.protection: Phase loss relay required for critical motors; trips on voltage or current imbalance

steps: 1. Identify all energy sources; include control circuits, capacitors, stored energy
3. Shut down equipment using normal procedures
5. Apply lockout device (each worker applies own lock)
6. Apply tag with name, date, reason
7. Release/dissipate stored energy (capacitors, springs, hydraulic pressure)
8. Verify isolation with meter; check all phases to ground and phase-to-phase
lockout_tagout.verification: Test for voltage with known-live source first, then dead circuit, then known-live again (prove meter working)

arc_flash_boundaries.limited_approach: 3.5ft for 480V; shock hazard boundary; unqualified persons must not cross
arc_flash_boundaries.prohibited_approach: <1 inch; same as energized work distance; avoid if possible
arc_flash_boundaries.arc_flash_boundary: Based on incident energy; 4ft to 10ft typical; full PPE required; calculate per NFPA 70E

ppe_requirements.category_1: 4 cal/cm² arc-rated shirt/pants; 120/240V panels up to 25kA
ppe_requirements.category_2: 8 cal/cm² arc flash suit; 480V work; typical industrial
ppe_requirements.category_3_4: 25-40 cal/cm²; full face shield; high energy work; avoid with de-energization
